title: Saxifraga dhwojii
taxonomy: Saxifraga dhwojii belongs to the family Saxifragaceae and is a member of the genus Saxifraga. This species is a type of alpine saxifrage and is native to the Himalayan region. It is classified as a perennial plant, typically growing in rocky areas and alpine meadows. Saxifraga dhwojii is a relatively rare species and is often found at high elevations. Its classification is further supported by its distinct characteristics, such as its small white flowers and grass-like foliage.

intro:
I apologize but I could not find any information on Saxifraga dhwojii. However, I found information on Saxifraga stolonifera, also known as Indian Saxifrage or Saxifrage. 

Saxifraga stolonifera is a plant species in the family Saxifragaceae. This perennial herb is native to East Asia, known for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Saxifraga stolonifera is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
